input.background: Among U. S. adults from 1999-2000, American Indians/Alaska Natives had the greatest prevalence of smoking (p1=0.40); Chinese-Americans had the lowest prevalence (p2=0.12). Suppose we take an SRS of 1000 individuals from each of these groups (n1=n2=1000).
input.question: What is the probability a sample will show a prevalence difference of 0.26 or less?

output.method: 事件发生概率及独立性

p1.description: Prevalence of smoking among American Indians/Alaska Natives

n1.description: Sample size of American Indians/Alaska Natives

prevalence_difference.description: The observed difference in smoking prevalence between the two groups

meta_info.caption: Prevalence of cigarette use in two ethnic groups.
